Phase 1 complete. Build scope confirmed and created. Phase 1 recap - Scope ID: 4qjsyw1jd4k0r75gy42wq2vynq - Route: /scope/jpmorgan-chase-fortress-communications-hub - Name: JPMorgan Chase Fortress Communications Hub - Welcome message set - Channel creation policy: channel_creation_by_admins_only = true Phase 2 — BUILD CHANNELS with FAQs (completed) CHANNEL_MAP - Ch1: Executive Updates → 44w9aj1etyzy01dwhgg24ms5x6 [PRIMARY] - Ch2: Fortress Risk & Capital → 47rjhv3mrs69a7z62gwmgtg049 [PRIMARY] - Ch3: CIB Markets & Client Coverage → 4ys8mcvxxe2ph49n6da9r0hf58 [PRIMARY] - Ch4: Technology & Innovation → 4gaaeb5mabhc762yafnx8gj0xf [PRIMARY] - Ch5: Compliance & Legal Policy → 4pwnhd07vjwqpxzv8xxqq420wc [SECONDARY] - Ch6: People, Careers & Culture → 4gdwj3frn72zes002ayegy914b [SECONDARY] Notes - Primary channels (Ch1–Ch4) were created with is_sticky = true. - Secondary channels (Ch5–Ch6) were created with is_sticky = false. - FAQs were added for Ch5 and Ch6 as part of their creation (policy/legal requirements and talent/culture queries). - Image_url for each channel set to the provided CMS assets: - Ch1: jp/leadership_speech.png - Ch2: jp/risk.png - Ch3: jp/stock_market.png - Ch4: jp/hacker.png - Ch5: jp/law.png - Ch6: jp/community.png - Description fields include HTML-rich content per your Phase 2 plan. - Channel colors align with the design (primary, secondary, warn, etc.). Seeded content plan (Phase 2 groundwork) - Primary channels (Ch1–Ch4): planned seed articles - Ch1: 3 articles - Ch2: 3 articles - Ch3: 2 articles - Ch4: 3 articles - Secondary channels (Ch5–Ch6): planned seed articles - Ch5: 4 articles - Ch6: 4 articles Phase 3 — POPULATE CONTENT (next) Before I write articles, I need your confirmation to proceed with Phase 3. Here’s what I’ll do once you approve: What I’ll do in Phase 3 - Print CHANNEL_MAP in full (as above) to ensure IDs are correct for article creation. - For each channel, create articles with the exact fields: - channel_id: from CHANNEL_MAP - title: specific, newsy, and aligned to JPMorgan’s voice - body: HTML, 6–8 paragraphs minimum, structured with

,

,

,

    /
      , ,
      , as needed - status: Published - tags: 3–5 precise tags per article - is_main_highlight: True for 3–4 hub-highlight articles total (across the hub) - is_sticky: True for 1–2 pinned articles per channel (as per design) - require_read_confirmation: True for all policy/compliance/legal/IT-security articles - notify: True for major announcements and policies; False for evergreen/guides/spotlights - hide_likes / hide_comments: False (default engagement enabled) - Each article’s body will reference JPMorgan-specific facts from the profile (revenue figures, product names, office locations, leadership, milestones, etc.) and match the formal, authoritative JPMorgan voice. - Track per-channel counts to ensure PRIMARY channels end with 3–5 articles and SECONDARY 2–3 (per your design). - After finishing each channel, I’ll print: “Ch[N] [name] — DONE: [X] articles created ✓” and move to the next channel without awaiting further confirmation. - After all channels, I’ll report total article counts and any policy fulfillments (e.g., number of policy articles with require_read_confirmation). Phase 3 status cue - CHANNEL_MAP is set and channels exist. - Ready to seed content per your plan. - Please confirm: “Proceed to Phase 3: Populate content with seeded articles.” - If you’d like any tweaks (e.g., adjust article counts per channel, tweak titles, or add/balance highlight picks), tell me now and I’ll adjust before writing. Shall I proceed with Phase 3: Populate Content?

arguments.name: JPMorgan Chase Fortress Communications Hub
arguments.description: Workspace hub for internal corporate communications across CIB, CCB, CB, and AWM reflecting Fortress Balance Sheet philosophy; anchored in risk management, compliance, and regulatory clarity.
arguments.welcome_message: Welcome to the Fortress Communications Hub. This space delivers precise, fact-based internal communications across CIB, CCB, CB, and AWM, anchored in risk awareness and regulatory clarity to support informed decision-making at every level.

arguments.name: Executive Updates
arguments.description: Official leadership communications, quarterly updates, and strategic memos from the CEO and executive committee. These messages frame the firm's priorities, risk posture, and governance expectations for the year ahead.

arguments.name: Fortress Risk & Capital
arguments.description: Risk governance, capital adequacy, and regulatory framing. This channel provides authoritative explanations of risk metrics, capital positions, and governance decisions that impact planning and operations across the firm.

arguments.name: CIB Markets & Client Coverage
arguments.description: Market insights, product coverage updates, client coverage strategy, and league-table context. Content focuses on performance metrics, market outlooks, and cross-divisional collaboration.

arguments.name: Technology & Innovation
arguments.description: Technology posture, AI initiatives, and platform updates (COIN, Athena, Fusion, LLM Suite, Onyx). This channel communicates tech strategy and innovation milestones across the firm.

arguments.name: Compliance & Legal Policy
arguments.description: Regulatory and policy communications; policy references, legal obligations, and risk controls. This channel houses mandatory policy updates and training materials.

frequent_questions.question: What is Information Barrier Policy (Chinese Walls) and why is it mandatory?
frequent_questions.answer: Information barriers prohibit sharing MNPI between business units; annual awareness, training, and governance review are required. Consult Legal/Compliance for any uncertainties.

frequent_questions.question: Who must complete annual information barrier training?
frequent_questions.answer: All employees; training schedules are managed through the LMS, with manager enforcement for compliance.

frequent_questions.question: What should I do if I'm unsure about permissible communication?
frequent_questions.answer: Consult Legal/Compliance before communicating; follow the escalation path outlined in the policy.

frequent_questions.question: Where can I find the mandatory acknowledgement article?
frequent_questions.answer: The article is published in this channel and must be acknowledged as part of the read-confirmation workflow.

arguments.name: People, Careers & Culture
arguments.description: Talent development, apprenticeship programs, DEI initiatives, and culture updates that reflect JPMorgan's commitment to workforce development and inclusion. This channel hosts stories, programs, and mobility resources for employees.

frequent_questions.question: What is New Skills at Work and how does it affect my development?
frequent_questions.answer: The program supports workforce development with regional reach; employees can participate through manager guidance and LMS enrollment.

frequent_questions.question: How can I apply to the ReEntry Programme?
frequent_questions.answer: Eligibility criteria and enrollment steps are described in HR communications and the hub's program pages.

frequent_questions.question: What mobility opportunities exist within JPMorgan?
frequent_questions.answer: Internal mobility programs and mentorship opportunities are available to high-potential staff seeking growth.

frequent_questions.question: How can I access apprenticeships?
frequent_questions.answer: Details and enrollment are coordinated through HR and Talent Development teams; refer to hub program pages.

frequent_questions.question: Are there DEI initiatives I can join?
frequent_questions.answer: Yes — initiatives including Advancing Black Pathways and related programs offer participation and impact opportunities.
